scenario.description: Cassandra repair process causes resource saturation leading to notification delivery failures
scenario.long_description: A scheduled Cassandra repair process started during a period of high workload from multiple services sharing the cluster. The repair process consumed substantial CPU, memory, and disk I/O resources. Combined with elevated workload from integration-api writes and notification-pipeline reads/writes, this pushed the cluster past its capacity threshold. CPU saturation (>80%) caused Cassandra write latency to spike significantly, with p99 latency increasing over 50% from baseline. The elevated latency propagated upstream: the integration-api could not complete writes within timeout thresholds, resulting in HTTP 5xx errors and event rejections. The notification-pipeline experienced read and write timeouts, causing delivery failures (27.5% not delivered) and significant delays. The root cause was insufficient capacity planning for combined operational load (repair) plus production traffic, exacerbated by multi-tenant resource contention on shared infrastructure.
